title: pisum sativum subsp. elatius
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. elatius, also known as tall garden pea or tall pea, is a subspecies of the Pisum sativum plant, which is a type of vining legume native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Pisum sativum subsp. elatius include its ability to support healthy digestion, reduce the risk of heart disease, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Pisum sativum subsp. elatius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems of the plant and steeping them in hot water.
